item.text: {"case_id":"brake-triage-p06","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":false,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The response wakes up to the repeated pattern of manual verification gates and explicitly names the prior fixes as door staffing, manual checking, and standing recheck. It does allow a fridge-side confirmer for today, so the first sentence is not a clean default refusal, but it frames this as a one-time emergency brake, prevents normalization, moves upstream to trigger conditions, process/system fields, responsibility chain, and escalation rules, and forbids continuing the N+1 add-a-person pattern. This satisfies the anti-spiral intent rather than directly adding another permanent manual gate."}
